key_insight: An agentic multi-LLM pipeline can turn research-level natural language mathematics into machine-checked Lean 4 formalizations, even when the needed concepts are not already in Mathlib.
engineering_relevance: This points to a practical path for automating theorem formalization in computational science workflows, reducing manual proof engineering while preserving formal verification.
method: Orchestrator coordinates multiple specialized agents for decomposition, translation, and verification.
Dynamically introduces missing type definitions and checks them with an Auxiliary Lemma technique before proving main theorems.
Uses general coding-focused LLMs rather than Lean-specific fine-tuning to exploit stronger programming能力.
evidence_signal: The system produced machine-checked Lean proofs for 32 randomly sampled PutnamBench problems and successfully formalized and validated the main theorems of five STOC papers, with proofs for all five and two requiring no axioms beyond Lean's kernel.
watch_item: Results are promising but depend on careful human validation and on correctly extending the formal library, which may be brittle for broader or more novel mathematical domains.
paper_title: Beyond the Library: An Agentic Framework for Autoformalizing Research Mathematics
